basic_details.job_title: Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for Court Officer, Computer Operator and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Court Officer, Junior Administrative Assistant (JAA), Computer Operator
basic_details.short_information: The High Court of Meghalaya at Shillong has released an official recruitment notification dated 14th July, 2026 inviting online applications from citizens of India for 3 vacancies across three posts: Court Officer, Junior Administrative Assistant (JAA), and Computer Operator. The advertisement reference number is HCM.II/109/2020-Rect/49. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the official website, www.meghalayahighcourt.nic.in, on or before 14th August, 2026 (1700 hours).

important_dates.raw: Notification Release Date: 14th July, 2026 Online Application Start: Not specified in the official notification Last Date to Apply Online: 14th August, 2026, by 1700 hours Last Date for Fee Payment via eChallan (Cash at SBI): Within 3 days from the last date of receipt of application

application_fee.details: Online Payment: ATM cum Debit Card issued by State Bank of India (SBI) or Net Banking for SBI account holders. Select "ePayment" and choose "State Bank of India" on the Meghalaya Portal page to proceed. Cash Payment via eChallan: Candidates may pay by cash at any SBI branch. Select "Payment across Bank Counter," generate the eChallan, take a printout, and make the payment. This payment must be completed within 3 days from the last date for receipt of applications. Fees once deposited are non-refundable and cannot be adjusted for any other purpose.

age_limit.details: As per the notification, a candidate for appointment to High Court service who is not already in permanent Government service must meet the following age criteria as on the last date fixed for receiving applications: | General Category: Not more than 32 years Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe (SC/ST) and Other SC/ST: Not more than 37 years Minimum Age: 18 years (no application will be entertained from a candidate below this age)

qualification.details: As per the notification, a candidate for appointment to High Court service who is not already in permanent Government service must meet the following age criteria as on the last date fixed for receiving applications: | General Category: Not more than 32 years Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe (SC/ST) and Other SC/ST: Not more than 37 years Minimum Age: 18 years (no application will be entertained from a candidate below this age)

salary_details.details: Note: These are the starting figures on the pay matrix as stated in the notification. Allowances such as DA, HRA, and other benefits are not specified in the official notification.

selection_process: The notification refers to candidates "attending the examination or personal interview
" which indicates a written examination and/or interview will be part of the selection process. However
and interview weightage are not specified in the official notification. Candidates should watch the official website for further updates on this.
It is confirmed that no TA/DA (Travelling Allowance/Dearness Allowance) will be paid to applicants for attending the examination or personal interview.
how_to_apply: Click on the "Online Application" icon on the website.
First-time applicants must complete a one-time registration on the Recruitment Portal before applying.
Candidates who are already registered can log in directly and apply against the post of their choice.
Read all instructions on the web page carefully before filling the application.
Fill in the application form with accurate personal, educational, and contact details, ensuring the email address and mobile number are correct, as all communication will be sent online.
Pay the application fee through the applicable online or eChallan mode.
Submit the application before the deadline: 14th August, 2026, by 1700 hours.
Important: Applications received through any mode other than online will not be accepted.

content_sections.heading: Quick Overview - Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026

rows.details: Court Officer, Junior Administrative Assistant (JAA), Computer Operator

content_sections.heading: Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Notification Release Date: 14th July, 2026 Online Application Start: Not specified in the official notification Last Date to Apply Online: 14th August, 2026, by 1700 hours Last Date for Fee Payment via eChallan (Cash at SBI): Within 3 days from the last date of receipt of application

content_sections.heading: Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: As per the notification, a candidate for appointment to High Court service who is not already in permanent Government service must meet the following age criteria as on the last date fixed for receiving applications: General Category: Not more than 32 years Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe (SC/ST) and Other SC/ST: Not more than 37 years Minimum Age: 18 years (no application will be entertained from a candidate below this age)

rows.educational_qualification_required: Bachelor degree in Law from a recognized University
Bachelor Degree from a recognized University with Computer Knowledge
Bachelor Degree from a recognized University with Diploma/Certificate in Computers

content_sections.heading: Meghalaya High Court Salary/Pay Scale 2026
content_sections.text: Note: These are the starting figures on the pay matrix as stated in the notification. Allowances such as DA, HRA, and other benefits are not specified in the official notification.

content_sections.heading: Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Online Payment: ATM cum Debit Card issued by State Bank of India (SBI) or Net Banking for SBI account holders. Select "ePayment" and choose "State Bank of India" on the Meghalaya Portal page to proceed. Cash Payment via eChallan: Candidates may pay by cash at any SBI branch. Select "Payment across Bank Counter," generate the eChallan, take a printout, and make the payment. This payment must be completed within 3 days from the last date for receipt of applications. Fees once deposited are non-refundable and cannot be adjusted for any other purpose.

content_sections.heading: Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The notification refers to candidates "attending the examination or personal interview," which indicates a written examination and/or interview will be part of the selection process. However, the detailed selection process, exam pattern, syllabus, and interview weightage are not specified in the official notification. Candidates should watch the official website for further updates on this. It is confirmed that no TA/DA (Travelling Allowance/Dearness Allowance) will be paid to applicants for attending the examination or personal interview.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The list of specific documents required at the time of application or interview is not specified in the official notification. Candidates are generally advised to keep educational certificates, age proof, category certificates (if applicable), and a valid email ID and mobile number ready, since all official communication will be made online.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official website: www.meghalayahighcourt.nic.in. Click on the "Online Application" icon on the website. First-time applicants must complete a one-time registration on the Recruitment Portal before applying. Candidates who are already registered can log in directly and apply against the post of their choice. Read all instructions on the web page carefully before filling the application. Fill in the application form with accurate personal, educational, and contact details, ensuring the email address and mobile number are correct, as all communication will be sent online. Pay the application fee through the applicable online or eChallan mode. Submit the application before the deadline: 14th August, 2026, by 1700 hours. Important: Applications received through any mode other than online will not be accepted.

content_sections.heading: Important Links
content_sections.text: Official Notification PDF: Click Here Apply Online : Click Here Official Website: Click Here Official Notification Reference: HCM.II/109/2020-Rect/49, dated 14th July, 2026

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your online application for the Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026 is 14 August 2026. Ensure you apply well in advance to avoid any last-minute technical issues.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in this recruitment?
faq.answer: There are a total of 3 vacancies announced by the Meghalaya High Court for the posts of Court Officer, Junior Administrative Assistant, and Computer Operator.

faq.question: Q3. What is the minimum and maximum age limit for these posts?
faq.answer: The minimum age requirement is 18 years, and the maximum age limit is 32 years for all the posts announced by the Meghalaya High Court.

faq.question: Q4. Are there any age relaxations for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxation is provided. Candidates belonging to Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe and Other SC/ST categories can avail of an age relaxation up to 37 years.

faq.question: Q5. What are the educational qualifications required for the posts?
faq.answer: The qualifications vary per post: Bachelor's degree in Law for Court Officer, Bachelor's degree with Computer Knowledge for Junior Administrative Assistant, and Bachelor's degree with a Diploma/Certificate in Computers for Computer Operator.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for General/OBC candidates?
faq.answer: General and OBC category candidates are required to pay an application fee of Rs. 400/- for the Meghalaya High Court recruitment.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for SC/ST candidates?
faq.answer: SC/ST category candidates need to pay a reduced application fee of Rs. 200/- for the Meghalaya High Court recruitment.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your online application for the Meghalaya High Court Recruitment 2026 is 14 August 2026. Ensure you apply well in advance to avoid any last-minute technical issues.
